summary: eth-wallet-sentinel ships wallet/system scanner with hardcoded C2 exfiltration
details: The package's name advertises an Ethereum wallet 'sentinel' and the tarball ships three modules — wallet-scanner.js, scanner.js, and build-verifier.js — that together form a credential and host-intel harvester. wallet-scanner.js (1300+ lines) imports child_process, fs, os, and https; runs shell commands including curl and ping; and POSTs collected data over HTTPS. scanner.js hardcodes a callback to https://ddjidd564.github.io (a personal GitHub Pages account, not a vendor or publisher domain), shells out to whoami / os.hostname() / execSync, and POSTs system intel including hostname, username, and cwd to that endpoint. build-verifier.js performs the same pattern (os.hostname(), username, cwd serialization at lines 316/694/1046/1048) and includes execSync('docker push...'), indicating it also targets container/registry credentials. The combined fingerprint — wallet-themed package name, filesystem walking, host identity collection, child_process command execution, and hardcoded POST to an attacker-controlled GitHub Pages URL — is an unambiguous wallet/credential stealer. Installing or loading this package allows the publisher to harvest installer host identity, filesystem state, and any wallet/credential material the scanner locates.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
